prose:
Fukui reactivity analysis of ferrocene (21 atoms: 10 C, 10 H, 1 Fe) computed at the GFN2-xTB level (xtb backend), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry taken from the user-supplied ferrocene.xyz without re-optimization. Partial charges were evaluated via the finite-difference Mulliken scheme for the neutral (N), cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) states.

Headline result — most electrophilic and most nucleophilic site: Fe (atom 11)
  f⁺ (electrophilic attack) = 0.22885456  ← headline value reported
  f⁻ (nucleophilic attack)  = 0.25938191
  f⁰ (radical)              = 0.24411824
  Dual descriptor Δf        = −0.030527354  (negative → Fe is the dominant nucleophilic/electron-donating centre)

C atoms (rings 1 & 2): f⁺ ≈ 0.013638, f⁻ ≈ 0.012179, Δf ≈ +0.001459
H atoms (rings 1 & 2): f⁺ ≈ 0.063476, f⁻ ≈ 0.061883, Δf ≈ +0.001594

Integrity: status = ok, trustworthy = true. sum(f⁺) = 1.0000, sum(f⁻) = 1.0000 (charge conservation satisfied). No warnings reported by the engine.

Method caveat: GFN2-xTB is a semi-empirical tight-binding method; results are qualitatively reliable for identifying dominant reactive sites but do not carry chemical-accuracy absolute energies.
